problem_statement: EditTaskDialog.tsx is an oversized monolithic component that handles form state, validation, multiple field types, submission logic, and dialog chrome in a single file. Its complexity makes it the most error-prone component to modify. Decomposition into focused modules isolates form sections, validation, and submission logic for independent reasoning and testing.
in_scope: Analyse EditTaskDialog.tsx to identify distinct responsibilities and logical boundaries
Split EditTaskDialog.tsx into focused child components and/or utility modules (e.g. form sections, field groups, submission handler)
Use 200 LOC as a preferred target for resulting modules but do not force artificial splits where readability would worsen
Ensure the decomposed components compose to produce identical behaviour to the original EditTaskDialog.tsx
Apply the typed prop interfaces defined in P2-R2-06 to the new child components
out_of_scope: Adding new form fields or task editing capabilities — R2 is purely structural
Visual or design changes to the dialog — styling remains as-is
Changing form state management library or validation approach
Decomposing other components — each hotspot has its own task
requirements: EditTaskDialog.tsx is decomposed into focused modules with clear single responsibilities
Resulting modules target approximately 200 LOC each unless a larger module preserves better readability
All child components have typed prop interfaces
The composed result produces identical behaviour and rendering to the pre-decomposition EditTaskDialog.tsx
acceptance_criteria: EditTaskDialog.tsx is decomposed into clearer modules with preserved behaviour
No new features, visual changes, or API changes are introduced
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
The edit task dialog functions identically to the pre-refactor version including form submission and validation
dependencies: P2-R2-06 — typed prop interfaces for EditTaskDialog and its children should be defined
P3-R2-08 — shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages) should be available if used in the dialog
P3-R2-09 — apiRequest wrapper should be available for form submission
risks: Form state may be deeply shared across field sections, making clean decomposition of form sections more complex
implementation_notes: Natural decomposition boundaries: dialog wrapper/chrome, individual form sections or field groups, validation logic, submission handler. Consider extracting form logic into a custom hook (e.g. useEditTaskForm) to separate state management from rendering.
